prompt: Extract the worker pool into a reusable package

turns.content:
<task>
The worker pool logic is embedded in main.go. Extract it into a reusable pkg/workerpool package.
</task>

arguments.summary: Read cmd/server/main.go (46 lines). Worker pool: Job{ID,Data} -> startWorkers(n, jobs chan, results chan) -> Result{JobID,Output}. Uses WaitGroup, goroutine-per-worker, closes results after all done. Need to extract Job, Result, and pool logic to pkg/workerpool/.

next_steps.description: Create pkg/workerpool/pool.go with Pool struct, Job, Result types
Update cmd/server/main.go to import and use pkg/workerpool

turns.content:

ok  	goworker/pkg/workerpool	0.003s

arguments.summary: Extracted worker pool to pkg/workerpool/pool.go. New API: Pool.New(n, processFn) with Run(jobs) -> results. ProcessFunc type allows custom job processing. Simplified cmd/server/main.go from 46 to 26 lines. Builds clean, all 3 pool tests pass.
arguments.final_answer: Extracted worker pool into reusable pkg/workerpool package. Pool struct with New() and Run() API, configurable ProcessFunc. main.go simplified to use the package. All tests pass.
